Metadata.Case_Number: Originating Claim No 301 of 2022; Companies Winding Up No 195 of 2022
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Oon Swee Gek

Facts.Text: On 2012-03-26, Oon Swee Gek, a Singapore citizen, participated in incorporating Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd in Singapore together with her children.
From the incorporation of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd on 2012-03-26, Oon Swee Gek served as a director of the company.
From 2012-03-26, Oon Swee Gek was employed by Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd as Culinary Curator and Chef, responsible for culinary activities and staff training and development.

As at 2014-11-13, when a shareholders' agreement was entered into with MMM Delicious Private Limited, Oon Swee Gek held 40% of the shares in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd, which were later adjusted to 20% following MMM Delicious' subscription for new shares.
At the time of execution of the 2014 shareholders' agreement on 2014-11-13, Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd paid a monthly salary of SGD 5,000 to Oon Swee Gek, with corresponding employer CPF contributions.

On 2014-11-04, around the time of negotiating the 2014 shareholders' agreement, Oon Swee Gek prepared an internal email setting out her then-salary and proposed future salary increments linked to revenue growth for discussion with the incoming shareholder's representative.

Between 2015-01-01 and 2018-11-01, the monthly salary paid by Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd to Oon Swee Gek was increased on several occasions from the initial SGD 5,000 level, and these increases were implemented without prior notification to the incoming 50% corporate shareholder's representative.

Between 2015-01-01 and 2018-11-01, the aggregate amount of salary and employer CPF contributions paid by Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd to the three family members, including Oon Swee Gek, exceeded the aggregate amounts that would have been paid at the initial 2014 salary levels by approximately SGD 330,000, based on internal spreadsheets later exchanged between the parties.

On 2019-02-21, through her son acting as point of contact, Oon Swee Gek agreed in principle to a set of terms proposed by the incoming shareholder's representative, which included recording a SGD 1,550,000 amount in favour of Group MMM Pte Ltd in the company's accounts and changes to board composition and branding.
On 2019-02-26, Oon Swee Gek signed a revised shareholders' agreement for Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd together with the other existing shareholders and Group MMM Pte Ltd.

Around 2020-03-01, in response to cash flow issues during the COVID-19 period, Oon Swee Gek agreed that her salary from Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd would be withheld and accumulated as a debt recorded as owing from the company to her.

During the COVID-19 period around mid-2020, Oon Swee Gek, together with the other family shareholders, made an offer to sell their shares in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd to the corporate shareholder associated with Murjani Manoj Mohan.

In April 2022, through her son, Oon Swee Gek participated in a request to the representative of Group MMM Pte Ltd for reinstatement of monthly salary payments that had been withheld during the COVID-19 period.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, on the basis of the pre-2019 commercial arrangements and conduct, the subsequent shareholder and financing structure of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd should be altered or unwound in relation to the interests of Oon Swee Gek.
Party_Details.Rule: In shareholder disputes, courts generally assess whether the conduct between shareholders, viewed against their commercial arrangements and understandings, justifies remedies such as setting aside later agreements or ordering a buy-out or winding up.
Party_Details.Application: The incorporation of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd in 2012, the 2014 entry of a 50% corporate shareholder via a subscription agreement and shareholders' agreement, the initial modest salary levels for the three family members, the subsequent undisclosed salary increases between 2015 and 2018, and the 2019 restructuring of shareholder arrangements and recorded intra-group financing form the factual basis for assessing whether later relief should adjust the shareholding or contractual position of Oon Swee Gek.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judgment analyses whether the 2019 shareholders' arrangements were procured by economic duress or undue influence, evaluates the concept of commercial unfairness under section 216 of the Companies Act, and considers whether the appropriate remedy is a buy-out of shares, a winding up, or dismissal. It also addresses whether the proceedings constitute an abuse of process in light of a prior offer by the defendants to purchase the claimants' shares, and applies legal tests for economic duress, undue influence, and oppression to the detailed factual matrix, including findings on the parties' credibility and the nature of the salary increases and subsequent negotiations.

Metadata.Case_Number: Originating Claim No 301 of 2022; Companies Winding Up No 195 of 2022
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Tay Su-Lyn

Facts.Text: On 2012-03-26, Singapore citizen Tay Su-Lyn participated in incorporating Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd in Singapore together with her mother and brother.
From the incorporation of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd on 2012-03-26, Tay Su-Lyn served as a director of the company.

From 2012-03-26, Tay Su-Lyn was employed by Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd as Creative Director, Marketing Manager, and Events & Catering Manager, responsible for branding, business development, and event planning.
From incorporation until around December 2014, Tay Su-Lyn held 30% of the shares in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd.
At the time of execution of the 2014 shareholders' agreement on 2014-11-13, the shareholding of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd was adjusted so that Tay Su-Lyn held 15% of the company's shares.
At the time of the 2014 shareholders' agreement, Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd paid a monthly salary of SGD 1,000 to Tay Su-Lyn, with corresponding employer CPF contributions.

Between 2015-01-01 and 2018-11-01, the monthly salary paid by Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd to Tay Su-Lyn was increased on several occasions from the initial SGD 1,000 level, and these increases were implemented without prior notification to the incoming 50% corporate shareholder's representative.

On 2019-02-21, Tay Su-Lyn, through her brother as point of contact, agreed in principle to terms proposed by the incoming shareholder's representative that included changes to board composition under which she would cease to be a director of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd.
On 2019-02-26, Tay Su-Lyn signed a revised shareholders' agreement for Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd together with the other existing shareholders and Group MMM Pte Ltd.

Under the 2019 arrangements implemented on 2019-02-26, the role of Tay Su-Lyn in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd was described as Creative Director, distinct from her prior position as a director of the company.

Around 2020-03-01, in response to cash flow issues during the COVID-19 period, Tay Su-Lyn agreed that her salary from Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd would be withheld and accumulated as a debt recorded as owing from the company to her.

During the COVID-19 period around mid-2020, Tay Su-Lyn, together with the other family shareholders, participated in an offer to sell their shares in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd to the corporate shareholder associated with Murjani Manoj Mohan.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, given the pre-2019 shareholding, employment, and board roles of Tay Su-Lyn, later changes to governance and financial arrangements of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd should be adjusted in relation to her interests.
Party_Details.Rule: In disputes among equal or near-equal shareholders, courts consider whether subsequent changes to board composition, remuneration structures, and shareholder agreements are consistent with the parties' commercial arrangements and understandings, and may grant remedies such as buy-outs or setting aside agreements where appropriate.
Party_Details.Application: Tay Su-Lyn co-founded Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd, held a significant minority shareholding, worked full-time in branding and operations, and initially received a modest salary that was later increased without prior notice to the incoming 50% shareholder. In 2019 she agreed to revised shareholder arrangements under which she ceased to be a director while continuing in an operational role, and later agreed to salary deferrals during the COVID-19 period. These events form the factual basis for assessing whether subsequent relief should modify her shareholding or contractual position.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judgment evaluates how the 2019 restructuring affected the governance and economic position of the family shareholders, including the removal of two family members from the board and the recording of a substantial amount in favour of the incoming shareholder's corporate vehicle. It considers whether these changes, in light of the prior commercial arrangements and the parties' relationship, justify relief under oppression and related doctrines.

Metadata.Case_Number: Originating Claim No 301 of 2022; Companies Winding Up No 195 of 2022
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Tay Yiming

Facts.Text: On 2012-03-26, Singapore citizen Tay Yiming participated in incorporating Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd in Singapore together with his mother and sister.
From the incorporation of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd on 2012-03-26, Tay Yiming served as a director of the company.

From 2012-03-26, Tay Yiming was employed by Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd as General Manager and Managing Director, responsible for operations, dealings with banks and landlords, and developing business strategies.
From incorporation until around December 2014, Tay Yiming held 30% of the shares in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd.
At the time of execution of the 2014 shareholders' agreement on 2014-11-13, the shareholding of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd was adjusted so that Tay Yiming held 15% of the company's shares.
At the time of the 2014 shareholders' agreement, Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd paid a monthly salary of SGD 4,500 to Tay Yiming, with corresponding employer CPF contributions.

Around early November 2014, before execution of the 2014 shareholders' agreement, Tay Yiming consulted a lawyer on the draft agreement and conveyed suggested changes to the representative of the incoming shareholder.

Between 2015-01-01 and 2018-11-01, the monthly salary paid by Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd to Tay Yiming was increased on several occasions from the initial SGD 4,500 level, and these increases were implemented without prior notification to the incoming 50% corporate shareholder's representative.

By 2018-12-01, internal spreadsheets prepared for Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd showed that the aggregate amount of salary and employer CPF contributions paid to the three family members above the initial 2014 salary levels was approximately SGD 330,000.
On 2018-12-28, Tay Yiming informed the representative of the incoming shareholder that the total amount of salary increments received by the three family members was approximately SGD 330,000.

On 2018-12-31, after discussions with the incoming shareholder's representative, Tay Yiming agreed in principle that an amount of SGD 1,100,000, described as covering salary and CPF payments over a period, would be added to shareholder loans owed by Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd to Group MMM Pte Ltd and that Group MMM Pte Ltd would receive a portion of the total monthly salaries paid to the three family members.

On 2019-02-02, during a meeting with the representative of the incoming shareholder, Tay Yiming discussed spreadsheets showing total salary and CPF payments to the three family members and heard proposals to treat a larger amount as adjustments between the shareholders.

On 2019-02-19, Tay Yiming sent an email proposing that a SGD 1,250,000 amount be recorded in favour of Group MMM Pte Ltd in the accounts of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd and offered an additional SGD 300,000 top-up, while requesting that any conversion of unpaid amounts into equity not be implemented.

On 2019-02-21, Tay Yiming received an email from the representative of Group MMM Pte Ltd setting out terms including recording a SGD 1,550,000 amount in favour of Group MMM Pte Ltd, changes to board composition, branding adjustments, and future compensation arrangements, and he indicated agreement to these terms on behalf of the family shareholders.
On 2019-02-26, Tay Yiming signed a revised shareholders' agreement for Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd together with the other existing shareholders and Group MMM Pte Ltd.

Under the 2019 arrangements implemented on 2019-02-26, the board of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd was to consist of Tay Yiming and the representative of Group MMM Pte Ltd, with the latter designated as Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, while Tay Yiming's operational role was described as Director of Operations.

Around 2020-03-01, in response to cash flow issues during the COVID-19 period, Tay Yiming agreed that his salary from Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd would be withheld and accumulated as a debt recorded as owing from the company to him.

During the COVID-19 period around mid-2020, Tay Yiming, together with the other family shareholders, made an offer to sell their shares in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd to the corporate shareholder associated with Murjani Manoj Mohan.
In April 2022, Tay Yiming requested by email that Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd reinstate monthly salary payments to the three family members that had been withheld during the COVID-19 period.

On 2022-05-01, Tay Yiming received an email from the representative of Group MMM Pte Ltd declining to reinstate the salaries and stating that written employment contracts approved by that representative would be required for salaries to be paid.

On 2022-09-30, Tay Yiming, together with the other family shareholders, commenced Originating Claim No 301 of 2022 against Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd and the associated corporate shareholder entities seeking relief in relation to the affairs of the company.

On 2022-10-03, Tay Yiming, together with the other family shareholders, filed Companies Winding Up No 195 of 2022 seeking either a purchase of Group MMM Pte Ltd's shares in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd or a winding up of the company.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, in light of the pre-2019 commercial arrangements, salary practices, and subsequent 2019 restructuring, the later shareholder and financing structure of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd should be altered or unwound in relation to the interests of Tay Yiming.
Party_Details.Rule: When assessing shareholder disputes involving later restructuring of shareholdings and intra-group financing, courts consider whether the changes were entered into under acceptable commercial conditions and whether they are consistent with the parties' prior arrangements and understandings, and may grant remedies such as setting aside agreements, ordering buy-outs, or winding up.
Party_Details.Application: Tay Yiming co-founded Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd, held a significant minority shareholding, and managed day-to-day operations while receiving an initially modest salary that was later increased without prior notice to the incoming 50% shareholder. In late 2018 and early 2019 he engaged in detailed email and meeting communications with the representative of Group MMM Pte Ltd about the aggregate salary increases, the recording of a multi-million dollar amount in favour of Group MMM Pte Ltd, and changes to board composition and branding, culminating in the 2019 shareholders' agreement. He later agreed to salary deferrals during the COVID-19 period and initiated court proceedings in 2022. These events form the factual basis for evaluating whether subsequent relief should modify his shareholding or contractual position.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judgment examines in detail the negotiations between Tay Yiming and the representative of Group MMM Pte Ltd, including the escalation of figures associated with salary payments, the proposal to record a large amount in favour of Group MMM Pte Ltd as a loan, and the linkage of that amount to potential equity conversion. It applies legal tests for economic duress and undue influence to these interactions, assesses whether the 2019 agreements should be set aside, and considers appropriate remedies under oppression and insolvency frameworks.

Metadata.Case_Number: Originating Claim No 301 of 2022; Companies Winding Up No 195 of 2022
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: On 2012-03-26, Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore by three family members as a company operating a restaurant business associated with the name 'Violet Oon'.
On incorporation on 2012-03-26, the board of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd consisted of the three founding family members.
From incorporation until around December 2014, the issued share capital of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd was held 40% by Oon Swee Gek, 30% by Tay Su-Lyn, and 30% by Tay Yiming.
On 2014-11-13, Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd became a subscription agreement and shareholders' agreement under which MMM Delicious Private Limited subscribed for 50% of the company's shares for SGD 450,000.
Following the 2014 subscription, the shareholding of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd was 20% held by Oon Swee Gek, 15% by Tay Su-Lyn, 15% by Tay Yiming, and 50% by MMM Delicious Private Limited.
After the 2014 subscription, the three founding family shareholders continued to describe themselves publicly as founders or co-founders of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd.

By around 2016-08-01, the 50% shareholding in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd initially held by MMM Delicious Private Limited had been transferred to Group MMM Pte Ltd, a Singapore company wholly owned by Murjani Manoj Mohan.
In August 2016, Group MMM Pte Ltd appointed its sole shareholder, Murjani Manoj Mohan, as a director of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd.

Between 2015-01-01 and 2018-11-01, Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd increased the monthly salaries of the three family employees above the levels agreed at the time of the 2014 shareholders' agreement, resulting in aggregate additional salary and employer CPF payments of approximately SGD 330,000 compared to the original salary levels.
By 2018-12-01, internal records of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd showed total salary and CPF payments to the three family members from December 2014 to November 2018 of approximately SGD 1,279,980.

On 2019-02-21, terms were agreed in principle under which Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd would record a SGD 1,550,000 amount in favour of Group MMM Pte Ltd in its accounts, described as including salary and CPF amounts previously paid to the three family members and an additional SGD 300,000, with interest.

On 2019-02-21, it was agreed in principle that the board of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd would be reconstituted so that only Tay Yiming and Murjani Manoj Mohan would remain as directors, with the latter designated as Chairman and Chief Executive Officer.

On 2019-02-26, Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd became a revised shareholders' agreement executed by the three family shareholders and Group MMM Pte Ltd, which implemented the 2019 terms relating to shareholding arrangements, board composition, and financial arrangements.

Under the 2019 arrangements, the branding of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd was to emphasise 'VO Singapore' and include the words 'a Group MMM partnership' on logos and packaging, and public relations materials were to be revised to feature Group MMM Pte Ltd and its representative.

Around 2020-03-01, in response to the COVID-19 pandemic and cash flow issues, Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd and the three family employees agreed that their salaries would be withheld and recorded as debts owing from the company to them.

During the COVID-19 period around mid-2020, Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd received an offer from the three family shareholders to sell their shares to the Group MMM-related shareholder, which was not accepted, and the company continued operations with the family members remaining employed.

On 2022-05-01, the representative of Group MMM Pte Ltd informed Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd's operational management by email that monthly salary payments to the three family members would not be reinstated unless written employment contracts approved by that representative were entered into.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, based on the pre-dispute commercial arrangements, shareholding structure, and internal financial and branding decisions of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd, subsequent court-ordered relief should alter its ownership or lead to its winding up.
Party_Details.Rule: In company disputes involving equal or near-equal shareholders, courts consider whether the company's affairs, including share issuances, shareholder loans, board composition, and branding decisions, have been conducted in a manner consistent with the parties' commercial arrangements and understandings, and may order buy-outs or winding up where appropriate.
Party_Details.Application: Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd was founded in 2012 by three family members, with an equal or near-equal share split among them until 2014, when a 50% corporate shareholder entered via a subscription and shareholders' agreement. The company later increased salaries for the family employees, recorded a substantial amount in favour of the incoming shareholder's corporate vehicle in 2019, reconstituted its board to include only one family director and the incoming shareholder's representative, and adjusted its branding to highlight the partnership. During the COVID-19 period, salaries were deferred and recorded as debts. These pre-dispute events form the basis for assessing whether relief such as a buy-out or winding up is appropriate.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judgment treats Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd as the central corporate vehicle through which the parties' relationship operated, and analyses how the 2014 and 2019 shareholders' agreements, salary practices, and branding and governance changes affected the balance between the family shareholders and the incoming corporate shareholder. It considers whether these events justify relief under oppression and insolvency provisions, including potential winding up.

Metadata.Case_Number: Originating Claim No 301 of 2022; Companies Winding Up No 195 of 2022
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Murjani Manoj Mohan

Facts.Text: During the second half of 2014, Murjani Manoj Mohan became acquainted with the three family shareholders of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd.
By around September 2014, Murjani Manoj Mohan was serving as chairman of MMM Delicious Private Limited, a corporate vehicle he used for investments.

Between approximately 2014-09-01 and 2014-11-01, Murjani Manoj Mohan discussed with the three family shareholders the possibility that MMM Delicious Private Limited would become a shareholder of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd.

On 2014-11-13, Murjani Manoj Mohan, on behalf of MMM Delicious Private Limited, entered into a subscription agreement and shareholders' agreement with the three family shareholders of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd under which MMM Delicious Private Limited subscribed for 50% of the company's shares for SGD 450,000.
Following the 2014 subscription, MMM Delicious Private Limited, controlled by Murjani Manoj Mohan, held 50% of the shares in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd.

By around 2016-08-01, the 50% shareholding in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd initially held by MMM Delicious Private Limited had been transferred to Group MMM Pte Ltd, a Singapore company wholly owned by Murjani Manoj Mohan.
In August 2016, Group MMM Pte Ltd appointed Murjani Manoj Mohan as a director of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd.
From at least August 2016, Murjani Manoj Mohan was the sole shareholder, a director, and the Chief Executive Officer of Group MMM Pte Ltd.

At the time of the 2014 shareholders' agreement, Murjani Manoj Mohan and the three family shareholders agreed that the family members would continue to draw their then-current monthly salaries from Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd after the corporate shareholder's entry.

Around the time of the 2014 shareholders' agreement, Murjani Manoj Mohan indicated that he would not provide personal guarantees for institutional financing obtained by Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd, and personal guarantees for such financing were instead provided by the three family shareholders.

Around 2018-12-01, Murjani Manoj Mohan became aware that the monthly salaries of the three family employees of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd had been increased above the levels agreed at the time of the 2014 shareholders' agreement, and he raised this issue with them through discussions primarily with Tay Yiming.

On 2018-12-28, after being informed that the aggregate salary increments were approximately SGD 330,000, Murjani Manoj Mohan stated that the amount should be higher and proposed that an amount of about SGD 511,804 be added to shareholder loans owed by Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd to Group MMM Pte Ltd and that the family members' salaries revert to the original levels.

On 2018-12-31, Murjani Manoj Mohan proposed that an amount of SGD 1,100,000, described as covering salary and CPF payments over a period, be added to shareholder loans owed by Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd to Group MMM Pte Ltd and that Group MMM Pte Ltd receive a portion of the total monthly salaries paid to the three family members.

On 2019-02-02, during a meeting with Tay Yiming, Murjani Manoj Mohan discussed spreadsheets showing total salary and CPF payments to the three family members and referred to figures including approximately SGD 1,244,931.63 and higher amounts in connection with proposed adjustments between the shareholders.

On 2019-02-02, Murjani Manoj Mohan presented to Tay Yiming an estimate that the combined contributions associated with Group MMM Pte Ltd and himself to Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd had a value of approximately SGD 5,266,667, including amounts attributed to securing specific leases, contributions to the brand, salary payments, and introductions to bankers.

On 2019-02-02, using the estimated contribution figures, Murjani Manoj Mohan proposed that Group MMM Pte Ltd acquire 70% of the shareholding in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd in satisfaction of SGD 4,000,000 of the estimated value contributed.

On 2019-02-19, in an email to Tay Yiming, Murjani Manoj Mohan referred to approximately SGD 1,250,000 as the amount of salary and CPF payments received by the three family members over a period and discussed not pursuing this amount if new arrangements were agreed, while indicating that he was seeking a way forward that would avoid adverse consequences for the family members and the company.

On 2019-02-21, Murjani Manoj Mohan sent an email to Tay Yiming setting out terms including that Group MMM Pte Ltd would receive SGD 1,550,000 from Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd, that Group MMM Pte Ltd would receive compensation equal to the total monthly salaries of the three family members going forward, that public relations materials would be revised to feature Group MMM Pte Ltd, that the company's branding would emphasise 'VO Singapore' and include 'a Group MMM partnership', that two family members would be removed from the board, that he would be appointed Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, and that the roles of the three family members would be defined as Chef, Creative Director, and Director of Operations.

On 2019-02-26, Murjani Manoj Mohan, on behalf of Group MMM Pte Ltd, executed a revised shareholders' agreement with the three family shareholders of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd implementing the 2019 terms.

Around 2020-03-01, in light of the COVID-19 pandemic and cash flow issues, Murjani Manoj Mohan agreed with the three family shareholders that their salaries from Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd would be withheld and accumulated as debts owing from the company to them.

On 2022-05-01, in an email to Tay Yiming, Murjani Manoj Mohan declined a request to reinstate monthly salary payments to the three family members and stated that written employment contracts approved by him would be required for salaries to be paid, and he referred to salary payments from 2014 to February 2019 as 'excess salaries'.

On 2023-04-05, through his counsel, Murjani Manoj Mohan caused Group MMM Pte Ltd to make a written offer to acquire the three family shareholders' cumulative 50% shareholding in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd for SGD 6,000,000, on terms that included discharge of shareholder loans owed by the company to the family members, restrictions on use of the company name and 'VOS', and a 24-month non-compete in the food and beverage industry in Singapore.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, in light of the pre-dispute negotiations, agreements, and communications between Murjani Manoj Mohan and the family shareholders, the later shareholder and financing structure of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd and any buy-out or winding up relief should be adjusted in relation to his interests.
Party_Details.Rule: In shareholder disputes, courts consider the conduct and communications of individual controllers of corporate shareholders, including their role in negotiating shareholder agreements, proposing financial adjustments, and making buy-out offers, when determining whether and how to grant remedies such as setting aside agreements, ordering buy-outs, or winding up.
Party_Details.Application: Murjani Manoj Mohan entered the business in 2014 through his corporate vehicle, negotiated the 2014 and 2019 shareholders' agreements, proposed that salary and CPF payments to the family members be treated as amounts shareholder loans and potential equity adjustments, and later made a written offer in 2023 for Group MMM Pte Ltd to acquire the family's shares. These pre-dispute actions and communications form the factual basis for assessing whether and how court-ordered relief should affect his position and that of his corporate vehicle.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judgment closely examines Murjani Manoj Mohan's role in structuring the 2014 and 2019 arrangements, his presentation of figures relating to salary payments and alleged contributions, and his later offer to buy out the family shareholders. It applies legal tests for economic duress, undue influence, and abuse of process to his conduct and evaluates whether the 2019 agreements should be set aside and what form of buy-out, if any, is appropriate.

Metadata.Case_Number: Originating Claim No 301 of 2022; Companies Winding Up No 195 of 2022
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Group MMM Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: By 2016-01-01, Group MMM Pte Ltd was a Singapore-incorporated company wholly owned by Murjani Manoj Mohan.
By around 2016-08-01, Group MMM Pte Ltd had acquired from MMM Delicious Private Limited the 50% shareholding in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd that had been subscribed for in 2014.

From August 2016, Group MMM Pte Ltd, through its sole shareholder and director Murjani Manoj Mohan, exercised rights as a 50% shareholder of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd and appointed him as a director of that company.
Through its predecessor MMM Delicious Private Limited, Group MMM Pte Ltd's investment structure involved a payment of SGD 450,000 in 2014 for a 50% shareholding in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd.

On 2018-12-31, it was proposed in discussions that an amount of SGD 1,100,000, described as covering salary and CPF payments over a period, be added to shareholder loans owed by Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd to Group MMM Pte Ltd.

On 2019-02-21, terms were agreed in principle that Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd would record a SGD 1,550,000 amount in favour of Group MMM Pte Ltd in its accounts, consisting of SGD 1,250,000 associated with salary and CPF payments to the three family members and an additional SGD 300,000, with interest, and that any unpaid amount after three years or upon capital raising could be converted into equity at an agreed valuation.

On 2019-02-21, it was agreed in principle that Group MMM Pte Ltd's representative would be appointed Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd and that Group MMM Pte Ltd would have a casting vote at board level under the revised arrangements.

On 2019-02-26, Group MMM Pte Ltd entered into a revised shareholders' agreement with the three family shareholders of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd implementing the 2019 terms relating to shareholding, board composition, and financial arrangements.

Under the 2019 arrangements, Group MMM Pte Ltd was to be featured in public relations materials of Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd, and the company's logo and packaging were to include the words 'a Group MMM partnership'.
Under the 2019 arrangements, Group MMM Pte Ltd was to receive compensation from Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd equal to the total monthly salaries paid to the three family members from 2018-12-01 onwards.

Around 2020-03-01, Group MMM Pte Ltd agreed that salaries of the three family members would be withheld by Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd and accumulated as debts owing from the company to them, while Group MMM Pte Ltd remained a 50% shareholder.

On 2023-04-05, Group MMM Pte Ltd, through its counsel, offered in writing to acquire the three family shareholders' cumulative 50% shareholding in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd for SGD 6,000,000, on terms that included discharge of shareholder loans owed by the company to the family members, restrictions on use of the company name and 'VOS', and a 24-month non-compete in the food and beverage industry in Singapore.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, in light of Group MMM Pte Ltd's acquisition of a 50% shareholding in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd, its role in the 2019 financial and governance arrangements, and its 2023 buy-out offer, court-ordered relief should alter its shareholding or lead to a sale or winding up.
Party_Details.Rule: Courts assessing relief in shareholder disputes consider the conduct of corporate shareholders, including their acquisition of shares, negotiation of shareholder loans and equity conversion mechanisms, and buy-out offers, when deciding whether to order a sale of shares, maintain the status quo, or wind up the company.
Party_Details.Application: Group MMM Pte Ltd acquired a 50% shareholding in Violet Oon Inc Pte Ltd, negotiated the 2019 arrangements under which a SGD 1,550,000 amount was to be recorded in its favour and could be converted into equity, and later offered in 2023 to purchase the family's shares for SGD 6,000,000 with associated restrictions. These pre-dispute events form the factual basis for determining whether Group MMM Pte Ltd should be ordered to sell or buy shares or whether other relief is appropriate.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judgment evaluates Group MMM Pte Ltd's position as the incoming 50% shareholder, its role in the 2019 restructuring, and the terms of its 2023 offer to buy out the family shareholders. It considers whether the offer was reasonable, whether its rejection amounted to an abuse of process, and whether Group MMM Pte Ltd should instead be required to sell its shares or face a winding up of the company.
